contract.name: v2/disaster/object_class/spending.md
contract.description: Aggregates disaster and emergency spending by object class using DEFC filters, returning totals for obligations/outlays and optional award counts.

filter.description: Container for shared disaster filters; server enforces a DEFC list but tolerates other search keys without effect.
filter.constraints: Must include def_codes array of valid DEFC strings; additional keys like award_type_codes, query, and time_period are accepted but mostly inert.

filter.def_codes.description: Disaster Emergency Fund codes to aggregate over.
filter.def_codes.constraints: Required; array length >= 1; values must be uppercase from ['1','2','3','4','5','6','7','8','9','A','AAA','AAB','AAC','AAD','AAE','AAF','AAG','AAH','AAI','AAJ','AAK','AAL','B','C','D','E','F','G','H','I','J','K','L','M','N','O','P','Q','QQQ','R','S','T','U','V','W','X','Y','Z']; bad codes or non-arrays return 400 or 422.

filter.award_type_codes.description: Subset of award type codes to apply when spending_type is 'award'.
filter.award_type_codes.constraints: Optional; accepts arrays or even bare strings, including invalid codes, without changing results.

filter.query.description: Keyword text intended to narrow results.
filter.query.constraints: Optional non-empty string; empty string returns 422, but observed keywords did not alter aggregates.

filter.time_period.description: Array of time period objects with start_date/end_date/date_type from the shared search schema.
filter.time_period.constraints: Optional; accepted ranges (including far outside program years) produced the same totals as no time period filter.

spending_type.description: Determines whether totals represent all resources ('total') or award-only spending ('award').
spending_type.constraints: Required; allowed values ['total','award']; missing field yields 422 and other values return 400.

pagination.description: Controls paging and sorting (defaults: page 1, limit 10, sort 'id', order 'desc').
pagination.constraints: Optional; numeric entries may be provided as numbers or numeric strings and are coerced server-side.

pagination.limit.description: Results per page for parent object classes.
pagination.limit.constraints: Optional; accepts 1-100 inclusive (numeric strings allowed); values outside range return 422.

pagination.page.constraints: Optional; must be >= 1 (numeric strings allowed); out-of-range pages return empty results while totals persist.

pagination.sort.description: Field used to order parent rows.

results.description: Parent object classes with fields id (string), code (string), description (string), award_count (number|null), obligation (number), outlay (number), and children (array of similarly shaped objects). award_count is null when spending_type='total' and numeric when spending_type='award'.

page_metadata.description: Pagination metadata containing page, total, limit, next (number|null), previous (number|null), hasNext, hasPrevious; flags flip correctly for edge pages and empty slices.

totals.description: Aggregate obligation/outlay for the full filter scope; award_count appears only when spending_type='award'. Totals remain even when results is empty.

body.detail: Field 'filter|query' value '' is below min '1' items

quirks: filter.query must be non-empty, yet any keyword tested returned the same aggregates as an unfiltered request.
filter.award_type_codes is ignored: arbitrary values, including plain strings and bogus codes, do not change results.
filter.time_period objects are accepted but observed totals matched the baseline with no date filter.
Undocumented sort option 'total_budgetary_resources' is accepted but neither changes ordering nor surfaces that field in responses.
Totals are independent of pagination; empty result pages still include overall obligation/outlay sums.
risks: When spending_type='total', award_count fields are null and missing from totals, so typed consumers must tolerate nulls.
Empty result pages (e.g., high page numbers) keep non-zero totals, which can mislead aggregations if emptiness is treated as zero spend.
Silent acceptance of award_type_codes makes it easy to assume subset filtering succeeded when it did not.
Keyword and time_period filters appear ineffective, so downstream analytics depending on them may be misleading.
gaps: Purpose and behavior of the accepted 'total_budgetary_resources' sort key remain unknown because responses never expose that field.

probes.notes: pass - minimal total request works; defaults page=1 limit=10; award_count fields return null in results
pass - award spending exposes award_count metrics and five parent object classes
fail - omitting spending_type triggers 422 validation error
fail - invalid def_codes value rejected; error enumerates accepted codes
pass - DEFC QQQ returns an empty dataset with zero totals
fail - blank query strings are rejected
pass - even a string award_type_codes input matches the baseline award totals, indicating the filter is ignored

mismatches: Docs describe filter.query as a keyword filter, yet observed responses were unchanged for different terms and only blank strings are rejected.
Docs restrict filter.award_type_codes to a listed enum array, but the API accepts arbitrary values (arrays or strings) without effect.
Docs list sort options only through 'outlay', while the API also accepts 'total_budgetary_resources'.
Docs imply time_period filters results, but observed totals matched baseline even for distant date ranges.
gaps: Purpose and behavior of the accepted 'total_budgetary_resources' sort key remain unknown because responses never expose that field.
risks: When spending_type='total', award_count fields are null and missing from totals, so typed consumers must tolerate nulls.
Empty result pages (e.g., high page numbers) keep non-zero totals, which can mislead aggregations if emptiness is treated as zero spend.
Silent acceptance of award_type_codes makes it easy to assume subset filtering succeeded when it did not.
Keyword and time_period filters appear ineffective, so downstream analytics depending on them may be misleading.
